response: convert the art collection tax is calculated by applying the 5% discount to the 20% rate, resulting in an effective 19% rate on $2 million which equals $380,000. convert the securities portfolio tax is determined by deducting $100,000 from the $3 million value to get a taxable base of $2.9 million, then applying the 15% rate for a liability of $435,000. convert the original real estate tax is computed by applying 10% to the first $1 million, 15% to the next $2 million, and 20% to the remaining $2 million, totaling $900,000. convert the initial total estate tax liability sums the art, securities, and original real estate figures to reach $1,715,000. convert the corrected real estate value increases the original $5 million by 10% to a new total of $5.5 million. convert the new real estate tax calculation applies 10% to the first $1 million, 15% to the next $2 million, and 20% to the remaining $2.5 million, resulting in $1,000,000. convert the final adjusted total tax liability combines the unchanged art and securities taxes with the new real estate tax to equal $1,815,000.
